meta.title: Angular 21 Chatbot Content — Modular & Standalone
meta.description: Complete Angular 21 topic content for a chatbot using rich content types. Covers both NgModule-based and Standalone architecture.

suggestions.label: Show me a quick start example

contents.text: Angular 21 supports two architectural styles. Choose the one that matches your project:
Angular's docs currently recommend standalone components for new code, while NgModules remain important for existing and legacy projects.
contents.author: Angular Team

contents.title: 1. Angular Fundamentals
contents.subtitle: Applies to both Modular and Standalone Angular

contents.text: Every Angular project — regardless of module or standalone style — shares these core fundamentals.

items: What Angular is — a platform for building web and mobile apps
Angular project structure — src/, app/, assets/

contents.title: 2. Components & Templates
contents.subtitle: The building blocks of every Angular app

contents.text: Components are the primary building block in Angular 21. A component controls a portion of the screen (a view) via its template.

items: Lifecycle hooks: ngOnInit, ngOnChanges, ngOnDestroy, ngAfterViewInit

contents.text: Directives extend HTML behaviour. Angular 21 has built-in directives and supports custom ones in both modular and standalone styles.

contents.code:
Welcome back!

  • {{ item.name }}
...

Styled

rows: Add DOM behavior to any element

contents.code:
{{ today | date:'fullDate' }}

{{ price | currency:'INR':'symbol':'1.2-2' }}

{{ name | uppercase }}

{{ longText | slice:0:100 }}...

{{ data | json }}

{{ 0.75 | percent }}

items: Use inject() function in Angular 21 — cleaner than constructor injection
Optional injection with inject(TOKEN, { optional: true })
Injector hierarchy: Platform → Root → Environment → Element

contents.title: 6. Routing
contents.subtitle: Navigation for both NgModule and Standalone apps

items: Typed reactive forms — FormControl<string>, FormGroup<{email: FormControl<string>}>

contents.title: 8. Angular Signals
contents.subtitle: The new reactivity primitive in Angular 21

contents.text: Signals are the recommended state management primitive in Angular 21. They give fine-grained reactivity without Zone.js and integrate directly with templates and OnPush.

contents.code:
# Create new project (standalone by default in Angular 21)
ng new my-app --standalone

# Generate artifacts
ng generate component features/user-list   # or ng g c
ng generate service core/auth             # or ng g s
ng generate guard core/auth               # functional guard
ng generate pipe shared/truncate
ng generate directive shared/highlight
ng generate interface models/user

# Serve and build
ng serve                   # dev server
ng serve --port 4300       # custom port
ng build                   # dev build
ng build --configuration production   # prod build

# Testing
ng test                    # unit tests (Karma/Jest)
ng e2e                     # end-to-end tests

# Code quality
ng lint

# Add libraries
ng add @angular/material
ng add @ngrx/store

rows: Preview what would be generated without writing files

contents.title: 17. Real-World Architecture Patterns
contents.subtitle: Folder structure, smart/dumb components, auth flow

contents.code:
src/app/
├── core/                    # singletons, guards, interceptors
│   ├── services/
│   ├── guards/
│   ├── interceptors/
│   └── models/
├── features/                # feature-based modules/components
│   ├── auth/
│   │   ├── login/
│   │   ├── register/
│   │   └── auth.routes.ts
│   ├── dashboard/
│   └── products/
├── shared/                  # reusable UI, pipes, directives
│   ├── components/
│   ├── directives/
│   ├── pipes/
│   └── ui/
├── app.component.ts
├── app.config.ts            # standalone app config
└── app.routes.ts

rows: Fetches data, holds state, passes down via @Input
Receives data via @Input, emits via @Output, no services
Group files by feature, not type

contents.title: 18. Interview-Ready Quick Reference
contents.subtitle: Key Angular 21 concepts grouped by category

items: Components, data binding (interpolation, property, event, two-way)
Pipes: built-in, custom, pure vs impure
Routing: lazy loading, guards (functional), route resolvers
HTTP: HttpClient, functional interceptors, error handling
Change detection: Default vs OnPush vs Signals
AppModule, declarations, imports, exports, providers, bootstrap
Feature modules, Core module, Shared module pattern
Migration path to standalone using ng generate @angular/core:standalone
Component-level imports[] instead of NgModule declarations
Lazy loading with loadComponent and loadChildren: () => routes
TypeScript basics — types, interfaces, generics, decorators
Directives & pipes — built-in first, then custom
Routing — basic routes, lazy loading, guards
Forms — reactive forms with typed FormGroup/FormControl
RxJS — key operators: switchMap, debounceTime, combineLatest
Testing — unit tests, component tests with TestBed
Standalone Angular — bootstrapApplication, appConfig, component imports
NgModules & legacy modular architecture — for enterprise/legacy projects
Advanced — SSR, Angular Elements, i18n, custom decorators
